UW Grade Distributions(2016 Dataset) Part #2

Author

Khadija Saho

Published

January 29, 2026

Note: This assignment is turned in late but bring submitted with a accepted extension from TA.

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

Loading Data

uw_grades_original_df <- read.csv(
  "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kylethayer/Info-201-au-24-datasets/refs/heads/main/UW-Seattle_20110-20161-Course-Grade-Data_2016-04-06.csv",
  check.names = FALSE
)

Here: New Column Is Created

uw_grades_df <- uw_grades_original_df |>
  mutate(
    Program_Abv = str_split_i(Course_Number, " ", 1),
    Course_Number_Num = as.integer(str_extract(Course_Number, "\\d+")),
    Term_Season = str_extract(Term, "Autumn|Spring|Summer|Winter")
  )

Viewing the unique program abv

uw_grades_df |>
  pull(Program_Abv) |>
  unique() |>
  sort()
  [1] "A"      "AAS"    "ACADEM" "ACCTG"  "AES"    "AFRAM"  "AIS"    "AMATH"

Overall Grade Distribution Summary

overall_summary <- uw_grades_df |>
  summarise(
    Courses_Count = sum(!is.na(Average_GPA)),
    Avg_of_Avg_GPA = mean(Average_GPA, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Student_Count = mean(Student_Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_Course_Number = min(Course_Number_Num, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Course_Number = max(Course_Number_Num, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
overall_plot_df <- uw_grades_df |> filter(!is.na(Average_GPA))

Histogram

ggplot(overall_plot_df, aes(x = Average_GPA)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Average GPA (All UW Courses)",
    x = "Average GPA",
    y = "Number of Courses"
  )

Scatterplot

ggplot(overall_plot_df, aes(x = Student_Count, y = Average_GPA)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Average GPA vs Student Count",
    x = "Student Count",
    y = "Average GPA"
  )

Observation

Across the full dataset (after removing courses with missing Average_GPA), there are 39241 courses. The average of the Average_GPA is 3.41, and the average Student_Count is 45.38 students. Course numbers range from 100 to 750. From the histogram, most courses cluster around the mid-to-high 3.0 GPA range, and from looking at the scatterplot the very large classes tend to have more GPAs concentrated around the middle rather than the extreme high end.

Filter for comparisons

info_df <- uw_grades_df |> filter(Program_Abv == "INFO", !is.na(Average_GPA))
cse_df  <- uw_grades_df |> filter(Program_Abv == "CSE",  !is.na(Average_GPA))
summarize_subset <- function(df) {
  df |>
    summarise(
      Courses_Count = sum(!is.na(Average_GPA)),
      Avg_of_Avg_GPA = mean(Average_GPA, na.rm = TRUE),
      Avg_Student_Count = mean(Student_Count, na.rm = TRUE),
      Min_Course_Number = min(Course_Number_Num, na.rm = TRUE),
      Max_Course_Number = max(Course_Number_Num, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
}

info_summary <- summarize_subset(info_df)
cse_summary  <- summarize_subset(cse_df)
info_summary
  Courses_Count Avg_of_Avg_GPA Avg_Student_Count Min_Course_Number
1           288       3.561806          53.00694               100
  Max_Course_Number
1               498
cse_summary
  Courses_Count Avg_of_Avg_GPA Avg_Student_Count Min_Course_Number
1           666        3.43003          77.68318               100
  Max_Course_Number
1               599
compare1_df <- bind_rows(
  info_df |> mutate(Group = "INFO"),
  cse_df  |> mutate(Group = "CSE")
)

Comparison: Histogram

ggplot(compare1_df, aes(x = Average_GPA)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~Group) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Average GPA: INFO vs CSE",
    x = "Average GPA",
    y = "Number of Courses"
  )

Comparison Scatterplot

ggplot(compare1_df, aes(x = Student_Count, y = Average_GPA)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~Group) +
  labs(
    title = "Average GPA vs Student Count: INFO vs CSE",
    x = "Student Count",
    y = "Average GPA"
  )

Summary

INFO has 288 courses with a mean GPA of 3.56, an average class size of 53.01 students, and course numbers ranging from 100 to 498. CSE has 666 courses with a mean GPA of 3.43, an average class size of 77.68 students, and course numbers ranging from 100 to 599. The histograms show that INFO courses tend to be shifted slightly toward higher GPAs compared to CSE courses. The scatterplots suggest that CSE includes more large enrollment courses and shows a wider spread of GPAs across different class sizes.

Comparisons #2: Small vs Large Classes

Subset summaries

small_df <- uw_grades_df |> filter(Student_Count <= 30, !is.na(Average_GPA))
large_df <- uw_grades_df |> filter(Student_Count >= 200, !is.na(Average_GPA))

small_summary <- summarize_subset(small_df)
large_summary <- summarize_subset(large_df)


small_summary
  Courses_Count Avg_of_Avg_GPA Avg_Student_Count Min_Course_Number
1         21953       3.501909          20.53542               100
  Max_Course_Number
1               750
large_summary
  Courses_Count Avg_of_Avg_GPA Avg_Student_Count Min_Course_Number
1           962       3.011538          321.1861               100
  Max_Course_Number
1               442

All In One Dataframe

compare2_df <- bind_rows(
  small_df |> mutate(Group = "Small (<= 30 students)"),
  large_df |> mutate(Group = "Large (>= 200 students)")
)

Histogram (Small vs Large)

ggplot(compare2_df, aes(x = Average_GPA)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~Group) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Average GPA: Small vs Large Classes",
    x = "Average GPA",
    y = "Number of Courses"
  )

Scatterplot (Small vs Large)

ggplot(compare2_df, aes(x = Student_Count, y = Average_GPA)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~Group) +
  labs(
    title = "Average GPA vs Student Count: Small vs Large Classes",
    x = "Student Count",
    y = "Average GPA"
  )

Summary

Small classes (≤ 30 students) have 21953 courses with an average Average_GPA of 3.5, an average class size of 20.54 students, and course numbers ranging from 100 to 750. Large classes (≥ 200 students) include 962 courses with an average Average_GPA of 3.01, an average class size of 321.19 students, and course numbers ranging from 100 to 442. The histograms show small classes skew higher in GPA, while large classes have more values are spread closer to the low-to-mid 3.0 range. In the scatterplots, large classes cluster tightly at high enrollments with fewer very high GPA points compared to small classes.
